title: Smoky Dense Portuguese Shrimp Stew
description: A quick, smoky, and hearty shrimp stew with a rich tomato base and a hint of Mediterranean flair. The stew is thickened with a touch of shortening for a velvety texture and finished with fresh herbs.

ingredients.name: White wine
ingredients.notes: use dry white wine for acidity

ingredients.name: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Heat a large pot over medium heat and melt the shortening, then add the olive oil.
Add the diced onion and bell pepper, sautéing until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and smoked paprika, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and white wine, stirring to combine and deglaze the pan.
Add the fish stock or water and bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Introduce the shrimp, ensuring they are fully submerged, and simmer until they turn pink and opaque, about 5 minutes—do not overcook to keep them tender.
Taste the stew and season with sea salt and black pepper as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley and a drizzle of olive oil if desired.
If a thicker consistency is preferred, let the stew reduce for an additional 5 minutes or stir in a teaspoon of tomato paste while simmering.
